title: Add search and local persistence to the JSX triage board
summary: Introduced search and local persistence in the same JSX file, creating another overlapping session with a distinct rationale centered on continuity and retrieval.
goal: Expand the fixture into a more realistic React frontend by letting review state survive reload and by making large queues searchable.
changesMade: Added a text search field that filters the rendered task set
Persisted todos and filter state locally
Shaped the state to coexist with the earlier tab-based triage flow
Documented the persistence rationale for later overlap analysis

features.name: search-backed triage board
features.why: Large review queues stop being usable unless the frontend can narrow the visible set quickly.

features.name: local persistence
features.why: Reviewers should not lose work when they reload the page or reopen the fixture.

features.name: fixture story continuity
features.why: The session narrative should make it obvious why this work overlaps with tabs, edits, and bulk actions.

decisions.decision: Persist only review state, not transient draft edits
decisions.why: Saving half-finished inline edits would create a rough demo experience and blur intent.

decisions.decision: Search inside the current triage context before widening to all items
decisions.why: Users think about narrowing the queue they are already in before jumping to another status bucket.

openQuestions: Should persistence include active tab, search query, and selection together or keep some of them ephemeral?
resumePrompt: Continue the search and persistence flow in fixtures/react-todo-conflict/src/App.jsx and reconcile it carefully with selection and inline edit state.
